Prerequisites: Azure CLI authenticated with a valid subscription (az account show works)
Docker daemon running and accessible (docker version should not show daemon connection errors)
Azure Developer CLI (azd) available in PATH
Node.js, Python 3.11+, .NET SDK, Java, and Docker available as required by services

Steps.Description: Create a new azd environment with a unique name
Set Azure subscription for the azd environment
Steps.Command: bash -lc "azd env set AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID $(az account show --query id -o tsv)"

Steps.Description: Set Azure tenant for the azd environment
Steps.Command: bash -lc "azd env set AZURE_TENANT_ID $(az account show --query tenantId -o tsv)"

Steps.Description: Set Azure deployment location
Configure API LangChain JS resource existence flag
Configure API LlamaIndex TS resource existence flag
Configure API Microsoft Agent Framework Python resource existence flag
Configure MCP Customer Query service existence flag
Configure MCP Destination Recommendation service existence flag
Configure MCP Echo Ping service existence flag
Configure MCP Itinerary Planning service existence flag
Configure UI Angular service existence flag
Disable GitHub Actions integration flag
Relocate node_modules to a workspace-local directory and fix quoting bug that broke the find path
Steps.Command: bash -lc 'set -e; base=/workspace/.nm-tmp; mkdir -p "$base"; find . -maxdepth 5 -name package.json -not -path "*/node_modules/*" -not -path "*/.git/*" | while read pj; do d=$(dirname "$pj"); safe=$(echo "$d" | tr "/." "__"); tgt="$base/nm-$safe"; mkdir -p "$tgt"; rm -rf "$d/node_modules"; ln -sfn "$tgt" "$d/node_modules"; echo "  -> $d/node_modules -> $tgt"; done'

Steps.Description: Exclude node_modules and temporary symlink targets from Docker build contexts to prevent COPY conflicts
Remove node_modules symlinks and temporary relocation directory to avoid Docker COPY conflicts during image builds
Steps.Command: bash -lc "set -e; find /workspace -type l -name node_modules -exec rm {} + || true; rm -rf /workspace/.nm-tmp || true"

Steps.Description: Build ui-angular image in Azure Container Registry instead of local docker build (avoids silent npm install hang)
Steps.Command: bash -lc "cd /workspace/packages/ui-angular && az acr build --registry $(azd env get-values | grep AZURE_CONTAINER_REGISTRY_NAME | cut -d= -f2 | tr -d '\"') --image ui-angular:azd-$(date +%s) --file Dockerfile . --no-logs"

Steps.Description: Update ui-angular Container App using ACR and resource group from azd environment (avoid empty CLI query results)
Steps.Command: bash -lc "set -e; RG=$(azd env get-values | grep AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP | cut -d= -f2 | tr -d '\"'); ACR_NAME=$(azd env get-values | grep AZURE_CONTAINER_REGISTRY_NAME | cut -d= -f2 | tr -d '\"'); LOGIN_SERVER=$(az acr show -n \"$ACR_NAME\" --query loginServer -o tsv); TAG=$(az acr repository show-tags -n \"$ACR_NAME\" --repository ui-angular --orderby time_desc --top 1 -o tsv); az containerapp update -n ui-angular -g \"$RG\" --image \"$LOGIN_SERVER/ui-angular:$TAG\""

Steps.Description: Provision Azure resources and deploy all services using Azure Developer CLI
Ensure container images are built and pushed (azd deploy – idempotent verification after azd up)

VerifyHints: source: inferred from azure.yaml and infra/*.bicep (Azure Container Apps via azd)

Step 3/21 : RUN tdnf install -y tar ca-certificates git python3-pip jq zip unzip     dotnet-sdk-8.0 dotnet-sdk-9.0 && tdnf clean all

Step 5/21 : RUN set -eux;     case "${TARGETARCH:-$(uname -m)}" in       amd64|x86_64) NODE_ARCH=x64 ;;       arm64|aarch64) NODE_ARCH=arm64 ;;       *) echo "unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH:-$(uname -m)}" && exit 1 ;;     esac;     curl -fsSL "https://nodejs.org/dist/v${NODE_VERSION}/node-v${NODE_VERSION}-linux-${NODE_ARCH}.tar.gz" -o /tmp/node.tgz;     tar -xzf /tmp/node.tgz -C /usr/local --strip-components=1;     rm /tmp/node.tgz;     node --version; npm --version

Step 7/21 : RUN set -eux;     case "$(uname -m)" in       x86_64)  DOCKER_ARCH=x86_64  ;;       aarch64) DOCKER_ARCH=aarch64 ;;       *) echo "unsupported arch for docker CLI: $(uname -m)" && exit 1 ;;     esac;     curl -fsSL "https://download.docker.com/linux/static/stable/${DOCKER_ARCH}/docker-${DOCKER_VERSION}.tgz" -o /tmp/docker.tgz;     tar -xzf /tmp/docker.tgz -C /tmp;     install -m 0755 /tmp/docker/docker /usr/local/bin/docker;     rm -rf /tmp/docker /tmp/docker.tgz;     docker --version

Step 9/21 : RUN set -eux;     case "$(uname -m)" in       x86_64)  BUILDX_ARCH=linux-amd64 ;;       aarch64) BUILDX_ARCH=linux-arm64 ;;       *) echo "unsupported arch for buildx: $(uname -m)" && exit 1 ;;     esac;     mkdir -p /usr/libexec/docker/cli-plugins;     curl -fsSL "https://github.com/docker/buildx/releases/download/${BUILDX_VERSION}/buildx-${BUILDX_VERSION}.${BUILDX_ARCH}"         -o /usr/libexec/docker/cli-plugins/docker-buildx;     chmod +x /usr/libexec/docker/cli-plugins/docker-buildx;     docker buildx version

Step 11/21 : RUN set -eux;     case "$(uname -m)" in       x86_64)  COMPOSE_ARCH=linux-x86_64 ;;       aarch64) COMPOSE_ARCH=linux-aarch64 ;;       *) echo "unsupported arch for compose: $(uname -m)" && exit 1 ;;     esac;     curl -fsSL "https://github.com/docker/compose/releases/download/${COMPOSE_VERSION}/docker-compose-${COMPOSE_ARCH}"         -o /usr/libexec/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose;     chmod +x /usr/libexec/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose;     printf '%s\n' '#!/bin/sh' 'exec docker compose "$@"'         > /usr/local/bin/docker-compose;     chmod +x /usr/local/bin/docker-compose;     docker compose version; docker-compose version
Step 16/21 : RUN azd config set auth.useAzCliAuth true
